stories.headline: Midjourney just built a full-body ultrasound scanner
stories.blurb: The AI image generator company unveiled its first hardware product: a ring of ultrasound sensors that scans your entire body. CEO David Holz says they're opening a San Francisco spa to run the scanners, a sharp pivot from making AI cat pictures.
stories.why_it_matters: An AI art company is now making medical hardware — that's a wildly unexpected move into healthcare.
stories.original_headline: Midjourney goes from generating cat images to full-body ultrasound scans

stories.headline: Adobe ships AI assistants into Photoshop, Premiere, and Illustrator
stories.blurb: Adobe's Creative Cloud apps now have built-in AI chatbots in public beta. Each assistant is custom-built for its app — the Photoshop one knows layers and masks, the Premiere one knows timelines and effects. They answer questions, suggest workflows, and walk you through features without leaving your project.
stories.why_it_matters: In-app AI help could make pro tools less intimidating for new users and faster for veterans.
stories.original_headline: Photoshop and Premiere now have AI assistants

stories.headline: Cursor can now automate your code reviews and Slack replies
stories.blurb: Cursor just added always-on agents that watch GitHub PRs and Slack channels, then act on them automatically. The new /automate command lets you define tasks once—like triaging issues or replying to messages—and the agent runs them in the background. It also gained 'computer use' so it can click through UIs when needed.
stories.why_it_matters: Code review and support work can now run itself while you sleep.

stories.headline: Amazon will now sell its AI chips to anyone with a data center
stories.blurb: AWS is in talks to sell its custom AI chips — previously only available inside its own cloud — directly to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy pegs the market at $50 billion, positioning Amazon as a rival to Nvidia's silicon monopoly.
stories.why_it_matters: Amazon just became a chipmaker you can buy from, not just rent compute through.
stories.original_headline: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips

stories.headline: FERC just gave AI data centers a fast lane to the power grid
stories.blurb: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission ordered grid operators to prioritize data center connections, letting AI infrastructure jump the queue that typically takes years. But the rule doesn't solve the actual problem: there's not enough electricity being generated to power all these facilities.
stories.why_it_matters: Faster hookups won't help if the grid can't generate enough power for AI's appetite.
stories.original_headline: AI data centers just got a government-mandated fast lane to the grid

stories.headline: UK government is using Google AI to speed up housing approvals
stories.blurb: Google DeepMind built a prototype tool for UK planning officers to process housing applications faster. The AI analyzes planning documents and local policy to help caseworkers make decisions, aiming to cut delays in a system where applications can take months.
stories.why_it_matters: Governments are starting to deploy AI in actual bureaucratic workflows, not just pilots.
stories.original_headline: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning

try_this_prompt.title: The learning roadmap
try_this_prompt.prompt: I want to get genuinely good at something I'll describe below. I have about three months. Build me a learning plan with three phases: what to focus on in weeks 1-4, weeks 5-8, and weeks 9-12. Include one thing to build in each phase and one mistake beginners make that I should skip entirely.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: When you're done watching tutorials and ready to actually get competent.

daily_question: If you could give one AI tool direct access to one part of your workday, what would you connect and why?
